# gstack-framework

> A Claude Code skill pack that transforms any repo to G-Stack quality.

## What this does

Point this framework at any project and it will analyze, audit, create skills, apply templates, and verify -- bringing your repo up to the standard of Garry Tan's gstack.

## Quick start

Install: git clone <repo> ~/.claude/skills/gstack-framework && cd ~/.claude/skills/gstack-framework && ./setup
Run: /gstack-framework (in any project)

## Skills (8)

- /gstack-framework -- orchestrator, chains all skills
- /gf-analyze -- scan repo structure, tech stack, docs, skills
- /gf-audit -- score repo 0-100 against G-Stack standards
- /gf-find-skills -- identify skill gaps across dev lifecycle
- /gf-find-agents -- find multi-agent chaining opportunities
- /gf-create-skills -- generate SKILL.md files for each gap
- /gf-apply -- apply ETHOS.md, CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, etc.
- /gf-verify -- verify everything, produce final score

## Templates (9)

SKILL.md.tmpl, CLAUDE-MD.tmpl, ARCHITECTURE.md.tmpl, ETHOS.md.tmpl, frontmatter.md.tmpl, preamble.md.tmpl, voice.md.tmpl, completion-status.md.tmpl, ask-user-question.md.tmpl

All templates use {{PLACEHOLDER}} syntax. Skills read and fill them.

## Checklists (3)

- skill-quality.md -- evaluate individual SKILL.md files (~60 checkboxes)
- repo-quality.md -- audit entire repo 0-100 across 5 categories
- audit-criteria.md -- detailed scoring rules

## Key rules

- Non-destructive: never overwrites without asking
- Every generated SKILL.md must pass skill-quality.md
- Skills are standalone markdown. No build step.
- Templates are the public API. Changing placeholder names breaks the apply skill.

## Repo layout

skills/ -- 8 active skills
templates/ -- 9 raw templates
checklists/ -- 3 scoring rubrics
reference/ -- analysis docs + original gstack clone
setup -- one-command installer
